{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":327601,"duration_api_ms":328079,"ttft_ms":3752,"num_turns":41,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS** — CP00-262 does not block pack or goal closeout; no unresolved P0/P1.

CP00-262 is a Risk-B, 40-unit descriptor-only search-core failure-binding slice (RP07.P07.M04.S06→RP07.P07.M05.S20). Every claim I could check via Read/Grep/Glob reconciles: pack binding, deliverable/micro-title distributions, contract projection (deep-equal to code), tests, and the no-write/no-real-data attestation chain. Critically, the pack honestly keeps `production_ready: false` (descriptor-verified, runtime deferred to CP00-263), keeps Claude as a read-only reviewer with human final approval required, confines Hermes to no-write review packet/receipt shapes, and carries the full CP178+ forbidden-review-evidence hardening.

Only one finding — a non-blocking P3 about redundant path labeling in `changed-file-scope.json` (the pack's own manifest dir listed in two buckets). Nothing material is dropped.

The pack is internally consistent and its claims are supported by the actual implementation, tests, contract projection, and validation logic verified via Read/Grep/Glob.

Verified: (1) Pack binding registry.js:487-502 matches range/risk/unit_count and upstream CP00-261 / next CP00-263 (RP07.P07.M05.S21), with clean continuity from the prior commit (CP00-261 ended RP07.P07.M04.S05). (2) Deliverable distribution (failure_recovery 19, security_audit 4, implementation 6, hermes_evidence 4, fixture 2, test 4, claude_review 1 = 40) and micro-title split (Secondary Workflow Slice 20 / Permission And Audit Binding 20) reconcile exactly across prompt, baseline included_units, SEARCH_CORE_CP262_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:2458-2512), and validateSearchCoreCp262Coverage (validators.js:5589-5622). (3) production_ready is NOT overclaimed: manifest.json, review-baseline.json, and command-evidence.json all hold production_ready:false (status implemented_pending_review / pending_final_closeout); production_ready_flag is a named descriptor-verification string; the Hermes packet emits only a gated production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid). No production_ready gate is weakened. (4) No-write / no-real-data boundary: freezeCp262Result (service.js:3638-3687) sets writes_product_state, dispatch/runtime, lock/retry/rollback, audit, object-storage, and Hermes-receipt flags false plus no_real_data/synthetic_only true; NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION inherits the chain (silent_success_detected/promotes_claude_to_final_approval/claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation all false); the descriptor validator (validators.js:5624-5710) enforces these. (5) Claude is a read-only reviewer, not final approver: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true in code, contract projection (search-core-contract.json:30355-30360), and tests (model.test.js:1784). (6) Hermes patterns confined to deterministic review packet/receipt: emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_...no_write, SEARCH_CORE_PROGRAM_CONTRACT.descriptor_only=true. (7) CP178+ review hardening present: forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:670-682) covers auth failures, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, and missing refs; readiness auth-preflight passed with well-formed JSON. (8) index.js wildcard-re-exports all 7 required public exports; README has #cp00-262; contract validator (validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:1334-1434) deepEquals the contract failure_binding_slice_descriptor/requirements/attestation to the code and asserts handoff + no-write fields. (9) Changed-file scope: all 7 modified source files are in active_pack_implementation_refs; unrelated dirty files (.docx, ldip result, new-session-handoff.md) correctly preserved as out-of-scope; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty.

No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3 metadata observation about redundant labeling in changed-file-scope.json.
